title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what motivates people to take part in protests and activism and what effects their actions might have.

images.label: A
images.description: Thousands of protesters marching through a city centre, carrying colourful placards demanding climate action, chanting with passionate energy

images.label: B
images.description: Climate activists blocking a busy road by sitting down in the street, cars backed up behind them as police officers look on from a distance

followUp.question: Do you think civil disobedience is ever a justified way to draw attention to an important cause? Why or why not?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what influences people's willingness to vote and participate in politics and why civic engagement matters.

images.label: D
images.description: A long queue of voters outside a polling station on election day, people of all ages waiting patiently to cast their votes

images.label: E
images.description: A young person attending a political debate at a university, taking notes as candidates argue their cases on a stage

followUp.question: Do you think young people are less interested in voting and politics today, or have they simply found different ways to engage politically?
